items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: Sorting is the classic stage for Ω(n log n) lower bounds and the difference between comparison and non-comparison algorithms.

items.title: Searching & Binary Search
items.description: Sorting once (O(n log n)) unlocks repeated O(log n) lookups via binary search.

items.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
items.description: Heap-sort uses heapify + repeated heappop; size-k heaps deliver top-k in O(n log k) instead of full sorting.

items.title: Recursion
items.description: Mergesort and quicksort are textbook divide-and-conquer recursions.

items.title: Time-Space Tradeoffs
items.description: Mergesort is O(n log n) time but O(n) extra space; heapsort is O(1) space but worse cache behavior — pick by which budget is tighter.
